rationale: Both correctly validate the unet-scan addition as safe and independently converge on the same legitimate design nit (hardcoded default_components duplicates per-pipeline _dit_modules), which echoes the spirit of dsocek's ground-truth 'single source of truth' comment, though neither hits its specific mechanism (packed_modules_mapping/naming-conflict renames), so recall is modest for both. X adds a genuinely valuable, diff-grounded catch Y misses: text-encoder LoRA components are still silently unmatched, the exact 'zero-layer no-op' failure mode the PR itself describes, plus a concrete warn-on-zero-match suggestion. Y compensates with more exhaustive grep-verified evidence and two lower-value nits (docstring, integration test), but doesn't surface anything as sharp as X's text-encoder gap. Neither has a labeled LATENT GAP CHECK section.
